result.finding: Minimal security implementation. No authentication, authorization, HTTPS/TLS, or encryption configured. The application runs on plain HTTP. Database credentials are hardcoded in docker-compose.yml. File upload is validated by MIME type and size. No Spring Security dependency included.
evidence: pom.xml: spring-boot-starter-security is NOT included as a dependency
application.properties: no server.ssl.* configuration (plain HTTP only)
No @EnableWebSecurity, JWT, OAuth2, or authentication classes in source code
No @PreAuthorize, @Secured, or role-based access control annotations found
No AES/RSA encryption in application code
docker-compose.yml: database credentials hardcoded in plain text (SPRING_DATASOURCE_PASSWORD)
PhotoServiceImpl: input validation — MIME type whitelist (JPEG/PNG/GIF/WebP) and 10MB file size limit
PhotoFileController: no-cache response headers (Cache-Control, Pragma, Expires)
Templates load Bootstrap 5.3.0 from external CDN (jsdelivr.net) — potential CDN supply chain risk
values: Input validation: MIME type whitelist + file size limit (partial protection)
Secrets management: hardcoded credentials in docker-compose.yml (insecure)
